paragraphs.body: Title: The Pittsburgh in My Mind Main point: How different I portrayed Pittsburgh in my mind, and how influenced my view was by American media.

Exposition: I expected the city of Pittsburgh to be a very busy and crowded place, kind of similar to New York. I imagined the people running to get to places quickly, maybe even holding a phone while running or looking very busy. I also imagined the weather to look completely different than it was in Kuwait (my home country)

Conflict: I was right about one of my predictions or expectations, I was right about the weather being completely different than my home country. Everything else was completely wrong, I was wrong about Pittsburgh being similar to New York in terms of being loud, busy, and chaotic. Pittsburgh was the opposite, it was quiet, relaxed, and chill, Pittsburgh is still a very large city with huge buildings, but the vibe and atmosphere are completely different.

Rising action: Pittsburgh is similar to Kuwait City, which is the main city in Kuwait in many ways, one of which is that both are big cities that contribute hugely to the economy of their respective regions, Pittsburgh for Pennsylvania and Kuwait City for Kuwait. Both of the cities have huge buildings and modern architecture of skyscrapers. I would say that Kuwait City is busier and more chaotic than Pittsburgh, in Kuwait City you can speak both Arabic and English. The culture is also different in both cities, as both cities have long differing histories, Pittsburgh in steel making, and Kuwait in pearl hunting.

Climax: The comparison made me see Kuwait City as a very busy and chaotic place. When you compare Kuwait City to a city like New York then Kuwait City will look quiet and chill, but when you compare it to a city like Pittsburgh then it’s the opposite. I now see Pittsburgh as the chill and quiet place and Kuwait as the busy and chaotic city.

Resolution: The cross-cultural comparison made me realize that the world is such a huge place where every city (not country but city) has its own history that’s being preserved by its people, this made me learn a completely new culture, which is Pittsburgh’s steel city culture, because in Kuwait all our history was related to the sea as it was where we made all our living from trade and fishing. In terms of linguistics, being in Pittsburgh made speaking in English feel natural as I’m speaking it everywhere as opposed to when I was in Kuwait, where I would only use it when necessary. The comparison made me realize that I let the media influence my view heavily when it was showing exaggerated scenes in things like movies.

annotations.comment: 4 The topic is a great choice. I like your AI-generated images. Please integrate your first language into the narrative. Good job!
